what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter metadata stub that records an image attachment in the design-source channel, linking it to image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-36 and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter with no body content
Target is an image:// URI pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-36
standouts: The design-source channel association and precise UTC timestamp
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
ISO 8601 timestamp with explicit UTC offset
Image target expressed as an image:// URI
Minimal body with no additional prose
concerns: No descriptive text, alt text, or content hash for the image
The image:// URI cannot be resolved from this file alone
No recorded author or provenance beyond the timestamp
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry linking an image asset to a design-source pitch, useful only alongside the referenced image and surrounding channel context.
questions: What does the image at image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-36 depict?
Who added this attachment and why?
How does this image relate to pitch p-36?
purpose_vs_actual: matches: the file acts as an attachment metadata record, which is consistent with its path and frontmatter fields
intention_signals: To create a durable, timestamped ledger entry that associates an image asset with a design-source channel pitch for later retrieval and traceability.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to address channel-local media within an attachment ledger.
contribution: It preserves the attachment's identity, channel membership, media type, target location, and addition timestamp, which a fused system would need to locate and contextualize the image.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger entry for the design-source channel that links image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-36 with id att-4e48e45e and an ISO 8601 addition timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and later resolve an image attachment tied to a recent pitch in the design-source channel.
